rules: Implementation must be scalable and maintainable.
Maintain consistency with the existing architecture, folder structure, naming conventions, and coding patterns.
Always work on the main branch.
After a successful commit, remove the completed step from the plan.
Reuse existing components and patterns whenever possible.
Avoid overengineering. Build the simplest scalable solution that meets current requirements. Do not add abstractions, optimizations, or features without a clear need.

Wall junctions: adding or removing any junction type or join style must be trivial — classify junction kinds in one classifier and keep join styles in a registry (Record<JoinStyle, JoinResolver>); no junction switch-ladders in the renderer.

Wall junctions: any value an architect may need to change manually (join style, miter limit, end cap, thickness-mismatch alignment) must be a configurable setting in editor.store with a UI control — never hard-coded. Geometric-truth constants (SNAP_EPSILON, collinear tolerance) stay as module constants.
Reuse existing code first (computeTopology, wallAngles, wallGeometry, dimensionChains, wallLayers); create new files only when no existing one can host the concern.
Curved/arc walls are a separate shape variant and tool (a distinct option alongside the straight wall), not a flag bolted onto the straight wall.
No temporary, partial, or construction-incompatible solutions; every junction must be a geometrically correct solid suitable for takeoff, consistent with the construction purpose of the project.
Every junction step must update dimensions and dimension display in the same step (measurementReference faces, per-segment dimensions, and dimensionChains break-points), never as a later patch.
Wall height (WallShape.height) is carried in 2D now for future 3D: set it on creation, keep it editable in 2D, and preserve/propagate it through every junction and edit path — never drop it.
See .claude/skills/wall-junctions for the authoritative geometry/computation reference for all 20 junction types.

steps.title: Miter limit -> bevel (incl. acute-angle spike clamp)
steps.detail: Add joinStyles/bevel.ts; when mitre length exceeds miterLimit×half-thickness (sharp/acute angles), substitute a straight chamfer between the two face points.
steps.dimensions: Face endpoints follow the chamfer, not the clamped spike.

steps.title: Round join
steps.detail: Add joinStyles/round.ts: fillet arc tangent to both faces, radius from setting. Registry entry only; renderer unchanged.
steps.dimensions: Measure to the arc's tangent points.

steps.title: Collinear continuation + thickness-mismatch alignment
steps.detail: Collinear ends pass straight through (no mitre). On thickness mismatch, align faces per junctionAlign (flush-left/centered/flush-right) and build the transition.
steps.dimensions: Continuous run reads as one span when thickness matches; transition offset reflected in inner/outer chains.

steps.title: Mid-span connection (split host)
steps.detail: When a wall end lands on another wall's body, project onto the host (wallGeometry.projectOntoWall) and split the host at that t into two segments sharing a node, producing a real T. Preserve height/layers/category on both halves.
steps.dimensions: Re-trace computeRoomAreas (centreline graph changed); chain break appears at the split.

steps.title: Validation hints: overlap + touching-not-joined
steps.detail: Detect collinear overlapping spans (#13) and near-coincident faces with no shared node (#14); surface as non-destructive warnings / offer-to-join. Never auto-delete.

steps.title: Eccentric / offset joins
steps.detail: Add a per-wall lateral centreline offset; compute faces from the offset centreline so walls can join off-centre (e.g. wall on one side of a column).
steps.dimensions: Faces and chains derive from the offset centreline.

steps.title: Composite multi-layer junction matching (BIM)
steps.detail: Match each wallLayers band to its counterpart across the junction so layers wrap/meet correctly, not only the structural core.
steps.dimensions: Layer takeoff lengths follow matched joins.

steps.title: Curved / arc walls (separate shape + tool)
steps.detail: Add an arc-wall shape variant and its own tool (distinct option alongside the straight wall). Join a straight wall to an arc using the arc tangent at the contact node.

steps.title: 3D height consumption
steps.detail: Extrude each wall to its own WallShape.height in the lazy-loaded 3D scene; handle height-mismatch junctions in 3D. Keep height editing in 2D.
